lightBloom.note: Reactor core is THE light source of this composition. Amber bloom should contaminate the pressure vessel walls and spill onto adjacent panels
Seven-segment displays always bleed light — green glow around each digit segment
Red SCRAM button should have a menacing glow even when inactive — it is a Chekhov's gun

chromaticAberration.note: None needed — this is not a lens-based display. The aesthetic is physical instruments viewed directly

filmGrain.note: 0.025 is present. Make it warm-tinted — grain should be slightly amber (#ffaa2208) to simulate cheap Soviet-era CCTV security camera footage

instrumentGlare.note: 0.08 at top-left is specified but needs expansion: each round gauge should have its own specular highlight — a small white-to-transparent gradient at 10 o'clock position on the glass face, opacity 0.06

reflectionGlare.note: Very subtle directional sheen across brushed metal panels — suggests overhead strip lighting reflecting off control room panels

heatDistortion.note: Subtle heat shimmer emanating from the reactor core center — radial displacement filter, barely perceptible

dustMotes.note: 15 dust motes specified at 0.06 opacity. Good. Add 3 that pass close to the camera as large, blurred bokeh circles (8-12px) — depth perception

cameraDrift.note: Very minimal drift — this is a fixed control room camera, but the building vibrates from the turbines. Use low-frequency rumble movement

dramaticReveals.note: On composition start, the alarm annunciator panel should flicker on with a brief white flash — like fluorescent tubes igniting
On start, ALL gauge needles should swing from zero/resting to their current position — as if the panel just powered on

breathingAnimations.note: Pulse at period 90 is specified. Add a secondary faster oscillation at period 15 with amplitude 0.02 — the reactor has a fast heartbeat underneath the slow breathing
Flow speed should subtly vary — pumps are not perfectly constant

microInteractions.note: Jitter at amplitude 0.8 is specified. Add occasional larger jumps (amplitude 2.0) every 80-120 frames on random gauges — mechanical backlash
Each rod should jitter independently with different phases — they are not mechanically linked

transitionMoments.description: Radiation monitor Agent Bay flashes amber — at this moment, coolant-loop-3 outlet temp readout should briefly pulse brighter, and the event recorder should receive a new CAUTION-level entry

dataDensityGaps.region: gap between temperature-gauges and pressure gauges (x:1058, y:488-504)
dataDensityGaps.fix: Add a thin 'DIFFERENTIAL PRESSURE' readout: delta-P across core = 0.042 MPa
Add millisecond precision timestamps and sequence numbers (SEQ 0001, 0002...) for forensic log feel
Add SCRAM count display: 'SCRAMS THIS CYCLE: 0' and 'DAYS SINCE LAST SCRAM: 847'
Add GRID SYNCHRONIZATION indicator lamp and FREQUENCY DEVIATION readout (+0.001 Hz)
Add engraved panel labels — small metal nameplates above each instrument cluster with stamped text like 'PANEL 4C-12 // ZONE TEMPERATURES'. Use #3a3a3a background with #555 text, 1px inset border
Add UNIT 1 identifier badge and plant license number

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: Desaturated institutional gray with warm amber core — grade like Chernobyl HBO (cinematographer Jakob Ihre). Lift shadows to #1e2022, never pure black. Midtones should have a slight green-gray cast — Soviet-era fluorescent lighting

keyMoments.shift: Amber wash over the entire right panel area — alarm indicator lamp color bleeds into surrounding surfaces, opacity 0.04
Entire composition shifts to red-dominant: background becomes #1a0808, all green text shifts to white, red elements intensify 2x

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: Amber and green are well balanced. The coolant blue (#3388cc) is underused as an accent — add blue tint to the coolant flow lines and pipe borders
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: Brushed metal texture is specified. Add subtle warm gradient from center (reactor glow contamination): center #1e1c18, edges #1a1c1e — the reactor IS a light source in the room

typographyNotes.fonts: JetBrains Mono is a good modern choice but consider Roboto Mono for a more industrial feel, or better: B612 Mono — designed for aircraft cockpit readability, perfect for SCADA
typographyNotes.engravedLabels: The engraved style at 8px with letter-spacing 2 is excellent. Use text-shadow: 0 1px 0 #555 for the stamped metal plate emboss effect
typographyNotes.displayFont: Seven-segment readouts should use DSEG7 Classic font for authentic digital display rendering
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: The alarm text at 20px is appropriately prominent. Readout at 16px is good. Label at 9px is too small — bump to 10px. Add a 'nameplate' tier at 7-8px for engraved panel identifiers

continuousAmbience.sound: Deep 50Hz power hum — the reactor is always audible, felt more than heard
Rushing water/coolant sound, pitched proportional to flow rate — Loop-3 at 4650 is audibly slower than Loop-1 at 4820
High-pitched mechanical whine at exactly 50Hz — turbine spin tone

eventSounds.sound: Sharp buzzer — two short pulses (BRRT-BRRT) on each flash cycle
Soft mechanical tick — like a wristwatch movement
Dot-matrix printer strike — rapid-fire clicks for each character

silenceMoments.description: Momentary dip in ambient hum before the radiation monitor flash — the reactor 'breathes in' before the anomaly
